patch.value: Dogfood evidence: extension registerParser('list') injected limit=1 but pm list --json kept filters.limit=null and returned full counts. Root cause in wrapped action forwarding only patched user options objects, not Commander processed args/options pipeline for core commands.
Fix parser overrides for core commands without positional args
Parser overrides registered by extensions were not applied to core commands like list when invoked without positional args.
Core parser overrides affect built-in commands with and without positional args; regression test covers pm list override.
Install parser extension overriding list limit, run pm list --json.
filters.limit reflects override and result count is constrained.
override ignored for core list command.
Agent-authored parser extensions cannot reliably shape built-in command UX.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: pm context --limit 10; pm search 'parser override core command' --limit 10; pm list-open --limit 20; pm list-in-progress --limit 20. Net-new because no active item tracked no-positional core parser forwarding bug.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Failure reproduced in /tmp dogfood project via local extension parser override.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Wrapper-level action interception must preserve Commander processedArgs semantics for variadic/single-arg commands.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Implemented core parser-forwarding fix in wrapped action dispatch by replaying parser-mutated args through Commander processedArgs and syncing option values via setOptionValueWithSource. Evidence: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts (61 passed).

author: codex-agent

patch.value: Parser overrides now apply to core commands without positional args; integration regression test passes.

message: Close parser override finding after fix

author: codex-agent

patch.value: Patched wrapped action forwarding to replay parser-mutated args via Commander processedArgs and synced option values before invoking core handlers.
